revision_note.revision_focus: Three critical gaps identified and addressed

gap_1_ve_contradiction_unresolved.issue_description: Data contains contradictory Ve interpretation markers

contradiction_detail.version_B: SDIndex framework Appendix B references 'External AI Narrative Trigger' with potential Ve shift from ~6.0 → 9.0
contradiction_detail.current_status: Contradiction unresolved in both Claude and original record

gap_1_ve_contradiction_unresolved.claude_handling: Acknowledged caveat but treated as secondary methodological note
gap_1_ve_contradiction_unresolved.revised_severity: CRITICAL—This is not a documentation caveat but a LOGIC INSTABILITY
gap_1_ve_contradiction_unresolved.implication: If Ve is actually activated via AI narrative trigger (not self_time_projection), then entire Ve(5.5) score rests on unverified mechanism
gap_1_ve_contradiction_unresolved.resolution_required: Observer must formally specify: Was Ve(5.5) achieved through self-paced contemplation during site, OR through post-visit AI knowledge scaffolding? These generate fundamentally different phenomenological claims.

gap_2_llm_training_risk_underestimated.issue_description: Claude's recommendation treats LLM usability as 'solvable through annotations'
gap_2_llm_training_risk_underestimated.claude_statement: 'USABLE_WITH_ANNOTATIONS' — Include methodological caveats in training data headers
gap_2_llm_training_risk_underestimated.the_risk: LLM models cannot be reliably instructed to distinguish between pure phenomenological data and augmented/mediated data when both are presented in identical field_notes format

concrete_scenario.training_case_1: LLM learns from pure sensory vector without knowledge scaffolding (ideal)
concrete_scenario.training_case_2: LLM learns from this record, flagged with 'mediated:true' annotation
concrete_scenario.model_failure_mode: Model cannot distinguish whether similar future sensory scores derive from pure observation or AI-augmented observation; treats both as equivalent sensory ground truth
concrete_scenario.actual_risk: Propagates false equivalence between 'what humans naturally perceive' and 'what humans perceive after AI-mediated interpretation'

gap_2_llm_training_risk_underestimated.correct_approach: Not annotations, but SEGREGATION into separate training buckets with explicit schema tags that models must propagate forward

gap_3_sensory_resilience_schema_incomple.issue_description: Claude frames resilience as 'accurate but untested under crowding stress'
gap_3_sensory_resilience_schema_incomple.claude_logic: Sensory_resilience=exceptional is valid prediction but unvalidated by actual crowd stress-testing
gap_3_sensory_resilience_schema_incomple.deeper_issue: This framing misses the actual schema problem
gap_3_sensory_resilience_schema_incomple.the_real_problem: At Katsura, sensory_resilience and Vr measure DIFFERENT VARIABLES that are being conflated

variable_distinction.sensory_resilience_actual_measurement: Space's intrinsic formal richness and optical design capacity to maintain perceptual quality under stress
variable_distinction.vr_actual_measurement: Available degree of freedom for observer to self-pace movement and attention allocation
variable_distinction.the_conflict: At Katsura: physical space can maintain exceptional aesthetic richness (resilience=exceptional), BUT institutional guided-tour structure removes observational freedom (Vr=6, not higher)

gap_3_sensory_resilience_schema_incomple.claude_diagnosis: Correctly noted Vr(6) reflects 'institutional restraint' and slow imposed rhythm
gap_3_sensory_resilience_schema_incomple.claude_error: Did not recognize this as evidence that resilience and freedom are INDEPENDENT VARIABLES that can violate expected correlation
gap_3_sensory_resilience_schema_incomple.schema_implication: This suggests sensory_resilience schema needs revision: distinguish between formal-aesthetic resilience (space property) vs phenomenological-freedom resilience (access system property)

revised_certification_with_corrections.confidence_reduction_reason: Unresolved Ve mechanism contradiction reduces interpretive stability

formal_dataset_layering_framework.framework_title: SDIndex Sample Classification Schema v2.0 — Phenomenology Mode Taxonomy
formal_dataset_layering_framework.rationale: Moving from binary GOLD/NON-GOLD to multi-layer classification based on observation modality and knowledge-injection patterns

layer_1_pure_phenomenology.definition: Direct unmediated sensory observation with no external knowledge scaffolding, no institutional mediation of access, no post-hoc AI consultation during critical reflection periods
characteristics: observer enters site with deliberate knowledge suspension
no guided tours, no mandatory institutional pacing
no AI querying during or immediately post-visit reflection
layer_1_pure_phenomenology.sampling_examples: Ryōan-ji if visited alone at dawn without guide; ideal Zen temple garden conditions
layer_1_pure_phenomenology.ve_accessibility: HIGHEST—unmediated access to temporal consciousness possible

layer_2_mediated_phenomenology.definition: Direct sensory observation that occurs within institutional or systemic mediation structures (guided tours, access restrictions, time controls) but without external knowledge supplementation
characteristics: observer must navigate institutional access protocols
pacing and attention allocation constrained by external system (tour guide, architectural design perfectionism)
sensory data is authentic but occurs within bounded conditions
layer_2_mediated_phenomenology.sampling_examples: Katsura as currently visited (guided tour required); any heritage site with mandatory access structure
layer_2_mediated_phenomenology.ve_accessibility: REDUCED—institutional mediation creates barriers to temporal consciousness even when space has high Ve potential
layer_2_mediated_phenomenology.reliability_flag: YELLOW—institutional variables require schema tags; cannot be trained as if unmediated

layer_3_ai_augmented_phenomenology.definition: Sensory observation supplemented by AI-assisted knowledge scaffolding that fundamentally alters the observer's post-visit interpretation and emotional trajectory
characteristics: pure sensory experience occurs first (on-site or immediate impression)
post-hoc AI knowledge injection (biographical research, historical context discovery, thematic reframing)
observer's emotional_trajectory is substantially modified by AI-provided information
final emotional closure depends on AI-mediated understanding rather than direct sensory realization
layer_3_ai_augmented_phenomenology.sampling_examples: Katsura (Prince Toshihito biographical discovery via AI changes entire meaning-structure); potentially Ninenzaka if post-visit AI context altered interpretation
layer_3_ai_augmented_phenomenology.ve_accessibility: VARIABLE—AI can partially restore temporal consciousness that institutional mediation suppressed, but this is SUBSTITUTE Ve (via knowledge) not INTRINSIC Ve (via experience)
layer_3_ai_augmented_phenomenology.reliability_flag: RED—AI variable must be explicitly tagged; cannot be treated as natural phenomenological outcome

mode_breakdown.observation_phase: mediated (guided_tour structure controls pacing and attention)
mode_breakdown.reflection_phase: ai_augmented (post-visit Google search altered emotional trajectory)

formal_resolution_required.decision_point: Observer must clarify: Was Ve(5.5) achieved during site immersion or through post-visit AI reflection?
formal_resolution_required.if_site_immersion: Ve(5.5) represents constrained self_time_projection under mediated conditions; baseline unmediated potential unknown
formal_resolution_required.if_post_visit_ai: Ve(5.5) represents partial restoration of temporal consciousness through knowledge scaffolding; AI played constitutive role in final emotional arc
formal_resolution_required.consequence: Classification and training bucket assignment depend on this clarification

revised_llm_training_protocol.previous_protocol: Include as GOLD_SAMPLE with annotation flags

training_segregation.bucket_definition: Observations where AI-provided contextual knowledge fundamentally alters emotional trajectory and final interpretation
training_segregation.training_objective: Train models to recognize and weight AI-augmented phenomenology patterns; NOT treat as equivalent to pure sensory observations
training_segregation.schema_propagation: Model must learn to carry forward 'ai_augmented:true' tag when processing or generating similar records

cannot_use_for: Generating recommendations for 'what humans naturally perceive' at heritage sites
can_use_for: Understanding how knowledge scaffolding alters sensory interpretation
Modeling AI's role in cultural understanding restoration
Studying institutional mediation effects on phenomenology
Analyzing how post-hoc narrative reframes emotional experience

model_instruction_requirement.issue: Annotations alone insufficient because models cannot be reliably instructed to distinguish training buckets post-hoc
model_instruction_requirement.solution: Embed schema-tag propagation as mandatory architectural requirement in model
model_instruction_requirement.example: If input includes phenomenology_mode:mediated_augmented, output must include same tag in any derivative analysis; model trained to fail if tags removed

revised_sensory_resilience_schema.issue: Current resilience measurement conflates space-formal properties with observer-freedom properties

resilience_v1_formal_aesthetic_resilienc.definition: Space's intrinsic capacity to maintain perceptual richness and aesthetic quality under stress conditions (crowding, noise, poor light)
resilience_v1_formal_aesthetic_resilienc.measurement_independence: Does NOT depend on how observer accesses the space
resilience_v1_formal_aesthetic_resilienc.katsura_value: Exceptional—space's formal design is so precise that aesthetic quality would persist under crowd stress

resilience_v2_phenomenological_access_fr.definition: Degree of freedom observer has to self-pace attention, movement, and temporal engagement during actual visit
resilience_v2_phenomenological_access_fr.what_it_measures: Access system and institutional structure properties
resilience_v2_phenomenological_access_fr.measurement_independence: Does NOT depend on underlying space quality (beautiful space with no freedom = low access_freedom)
resilience_v2_phenomenological_access_fr.katsura_value: Low-to-Medium (6.0-6.5)—guided-tour mandatory pace prevents free temporal exploration

revised_schema_implication.old_assumption: High sensory_resilience should enable high Ve, therefore Vt(9)+Vg(9)=exceptional resilience should correlate with higher Ve
revised_schema_implication.new_understanding: Sensory_resilience measures formal space properties; access_freedom measures institutional properties. Ve depends on BOTH. At Katsura: high formal resilience but low access_freedom → Ve suppression despite space potential
revised_schema_implication.schema_correction: These should be measured and recorded separately, not conflated as single 'sensory_resilience' variable

ninenzaka_kyt_20260226_a.classification: Pure observation (no guided structure, no AI consultation) but within high-distraction environment
ninenzaka_kyt_20260226_a.access_freedom: High—visitor can move freely, set own pace, linger as desired
ninenzaka_kyt_20260226_a.ve_suppression_source: Environmental distraction (tourist cheerfulness, present-moment hyperstimulation) NOT institutional control

katsura_kyt_20260118_a.classification: Observation within guided-tour institutional structure + post-visit AI knowledge injection
katsura_kyt_20260118_a.access_freedom: Medium-Low—tour pacing and design perfectionism control attention
katsura_kyt_20260118_a.ve_suppression_source: Institutional mediation (pace control, design control) NOT observer capability
katsura_kyt_20260118_a.ve_potential_restoration_mechanism: Post-visit AI biographical knowledge partially restores temporal consciousness that access restriction suppressed

dyadic_insight_with_corrected_schema.previous_insight: Two records show Ve suppression through different mechanisms (tourism vs institution)
dyadic_insight_with_corrected_schema.corrected_insight: Records demonstrate that Ve suppression depends on ACCESS STRUCTURE more than SPACE QUALITY. Ninenzaka's freedom despite distraction maintains some Ve potential; Katsura's institutional control suppresses Ve regardless of exceptional formal properties. AI can partially compensate for institutional barriers but cannot replace direct phenomenological freedom.

dataset_stratification_recommendations.stratification_requirement: Classify all records by phenomenology_mode BEFORE finalizing gold sample designations

required_stratification_audit.action_1: Review all 9 records and assign phenomenology_mode: {pure, mediated, augmented}
required_stratification_audit.action_2: For each record, separate formal_resilience from access_freedom measurements
required_stratification_audit.action_3: Clarify Ve mechanism in any record where contradiction exists (like current Katsura)
required_stratification_audit.action_4: Document knowledge_injection timing for any AI-involved observations

revised_gold_sample_criteria.criterion_2_ve_diagnostic_clarity: REVISED—Ve must have unambiguous activation mechanism; contradiction disqualifies until resolved
revised_gold_sample_criteria.criterion_3_emotion_trajectory_integrity: Unchanged—but must explicitly note if AI is constitutive to trajectory closure
revised_gold_sample_criteria.criterion_5_methodological_transparency: REVISED—Must include phenomenology_mode classification and access structure documentation in metadata

tier_1_gold_pure.definition: phenomenology_mode:pure + high vector synergy + unambiguous Ve mechanism
tier_1_gold_pure.expected_characteristics: First-visit unmediated observation without AI consultation
tier_1_gold_pure.rarity_warning: Probably rarest tier; most heritage sites have some institutional access control or prior knowledge activation

tier_2_gold_mediated.definition: phenomenology_mode:mediated + high vector synergy + transparent access structure documentation
tier_2_gold_mediated.expected_characteristics: Honest observation acknowledging institutional or environmental constraints on phenomenology
tier_2_gold_mediated.value_proposition: Demonstrates how institutions affect sensory assessment; enables modeling of mediation effects

tier_3_gold_augmented.definition: phenomenology_mode:augmented + high vector synergy + explicit knowledge_injection documentation + resolved Ve mechanism
tier_3_gold_augmented.expected_characteristics: Sensory observation + AI-mediated interpretation where AI's role is transparent
tier_3_gold_augmented.value_proposition: Models AI's role in cultural understanding restoration; prevents false attribution of AI-generated insight to pure perception

mandatory_supplements: 1. Phenomenology Mode Classification Card (marking as MEDIATED_WITH_AUGMENTATION)
2. Ve Mechanism Resolution Form (observer must select: self_time_projection OR external_ai_narrative_trigger)
3. Access Structure Documentation (guided-tour constraints explicitly listed)
4. Knowledge Injection Timeline (post-visit Google search verified with timestamps)
5. Training Bucket Assignment (augmented_phenomenology_subclass with explicit schema tags)
katsura_final_verdict_under_revised_fram.training_instruction: Treat as exemplar of how post-hoc knowledge scaffolding can restore temporal consciousness in institutionally-constrained sites
katsura_final_verdict_under_revised_fram.dataset_value: ESSENTIAL for understanding AI's role in cultural interpretation; completes picture when paired with Ninenzaka (pure phenomenology with external distraction)

critical_feedback_integration_summary.feedback_quality: Exceptionally penetrating; identified three system-level gaps

gap_1_ve_contradiction.claude_stance: Acknowledged but treated as secondary caveat
gap_1_ve_contradiction.feedback_correction: This is CRITICAL logic instability, not documentation caveat
gap_1_ve_contradiction.action_taken: Elevated to 'UNRESOLVED_CONTRADICTION' requiring formal observer decision

gap_2_llm_training_risk.feedback_correction: Annotations cannot prevent model misuse; requires training bucket segregation and schema tag propagation
gap_2_llm_training_risk.action_taken: Restructured from annotation-based to segregation-based protocol with mandatory tag propagation

gap_3_schema_incompleteness.feedback_correction: Deeper issue: sensory_resilience and access_freedom are conflated; Katsura demonstrates institutional variables dominate physical variables

critical_feedback_integration_summary.system_level_recommendation: Dataset should move from binary GOLD/NON-GOLD to layered classification with phenomenology_mode and access_structure as primary schema elements

immediate: Resolve Katsura Ve mechanism contradiction (observer decision required)
Assign training buckets based on revised tier system
short_term: Implement formal_resilience / access_freedom split in schema
Generate phenomenology_mode classification cards for each record
Design training bucket segregation with schema tag propagation
medium_term: Publish Katsura as GOLD_SAMPLE–TIER_2_MEDIATED with all mandatory supplements
Publish dyadic analysis (Ninenzaka+Katsura) demonstrating Ve suppression mechanisms across access structures
